full_prompt: Design one representative e-NABLE Phoenix v3 proximal-phalanx finger segment + gauntlet tendon-anchor pair, a 3D-printed body-powered upper-limb prosthetic component compliant with the e-NABLE Device Library specifications. The finger proximal phalanx is a PLA 3D-printed part approximately 55 mm long, 18 mm wide, 15 mm tall, with an internal 2 mm diameter tendon channel from the fingertip joint down through the palm. Flexion is driven by a braided fishing-line or polyester tendon anchored at the fingertip and routed through the PIP and MCP pivot pins, reacting against a wrist-driven gauntlet. Design tendon tension is 30 N nominal with a 50 N maximum per e-NABLE specification (30-50 N range). Joint pivot pins are 2 mm diameter steel machine screws acting in double shear. Target material is PLA 3D-printed on an FFF printer with typical layer adhesion giving a conservative tensile yield of 35 MPa (PLA bulk yield ~50 MPa; 0.7 anisotropy factor on Z-direction strength) and flexural modulus ~3.5 GPa. Print orientation is with the length of the finger parallel to the build plate (Z axis = finger thickness). The part must sustain a 50 N tendon pull with a safety factor of 2.0, i.e. verified to 100 N pull, without part fracture or pivot-hole ovalization greater than 0.2 mm. Joint torque envelope: the MCP pivot must transmit up to 50 N * 30 mm moment arm = 1.5 Nm design torque. Deliverable is a single CAD part of the Phoenix v3 proximal phalanx plus a dummy of the MCP pin suitable for (a) linear static structural FEA at 100 N verification tendon load applied at the fingertip anchor with the MCP pivot fixed, (b) pin-bearing check on the pivot hole. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design the proximal-phalanx finger segment of an e-NABLE Phoenix v3 body-powered upper-limb prosthetic, 3D-printed in PLA on an FFF printer with print orientation parallel to the build plate so the Z-axis is finger thickness. Flexion is driven by a braided tendon routed through an internal channel and pivoted on 2 mm steel pins in double shear. Design tendon tension 30-50 N per e-NABLE spec; safety factor 2.0 so verify to 100 N, with pivot-hole ovalization <= 0.2 mm. PLA design strength 35 MPa (bulk yield ~50 MPa, 0.7 anisotropy knockdown for Z).

Envelope: ~55 x 18 x 15 mm phalanx with a 2 mm tendon channel and 2 mm pivot holes at PIP and MCP.

Load cases.
- LC1 tendon nominal: 30 N axial pull at fingertip anchor.
- LC2 tendon max: 50 N axial pull at fingertip anchor.
- LC3 tendon factored (SF 2): 100 N verification pull at fingertip anchor with MCP pivot fixed.
- LC4 MCP joint torque: 1.5 Nm (50 N at ~30 mm moment arm) about the MCP pivot.

prompt.title: e-NABLE Phoenix v3 proximal-phalanx finger
prompt.brief: Design a PLA 3D-printed Phoenix v3 proximal phalanx taking a 50 N tendon pull with SF 2 (verified to 100 N) and 1.5 Nm MCP joint torque.

load_cases.name: tendon_nominal
load_cases.application: axial pull on tendon channel at fingertip

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: PLA tensile yield (Z-direction anisotropy already folded in)

analysis_types: linear static tendon pull + joint torque
